title: carex capitata subsp. arctogena
intro: Carex capitata subsp. arctogena, also known as the Alaska sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Carex capitata subsp. arctogena include reducing inflammation and promoting healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Carex capitata subsp. arctogena can be prepared into herbal infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to treat skin issues or internally to support overall health.
